title: 'How to read cancer statistics' data-literacy explainer (no per-cancer medical claims)
context: With the accessible dashboards built (incidence-explorers-dashboard-010) and the statistical-honesty spec approved (incidence-explorers-stats-spec-004), the project can write the flagship 'How to read cancer statistics' data-literacy explainer. This explainer makes no per-cancer medical claims (medium risk; no oncologist sign-off required), targeting a general audience at approximately grade 8 reading level. It is the general health-literacy output that remains valuable regardless of whether a beneficiary partner is secured for the higher-risk per-site explainers.
objective: Write a plain-language (target: ~grade 8 reading level) data-literacy explainer explaining incidence vs mortality vs prevalence vs survival, counts vs rates, age-standardisation (including the standard-population comparability trap), confidence intervals and estimate uncertainty (including that GLOBOCAN represents modelled estimates, not exact counts), and screening/diagnostic artifacts — each concept illustrated with a provenanced dashboard figure. The explainer makes no per-cancer medical claims and carries 'general education' framing. It must pass the content-safety lint (no advice/diagnosis/prognosis phrasings).
acceptanceCriteria: Explains in plain language (target: ~grade 8): incidence vs mortality vs prevalence vs survival; counts vs rates; age-standardisation including the standard-population comparability trap; CIs and estimate uncertainty (GLOBOCAN = modelled estimates, not exact counts); and screening/diagnostic artifacts
Each concept is illustrated with at least one provenanced dashboard figure (DataPoint with resolvable Source); no assertion is made without a citation
Makes no per-cancer medical claims (stays medium risk); carries 'general education' framing, not 'not medical advice' patient framing (no individual patient guidance at all)
Readability: meets the ~grade 8 target on a standard readability metric and passes a human plain-language review
Reviewed and signed off by the Statistics reviewer and Maintainer (recorded)
output: A reviewed, published data-literacy explainer ('How to read cancer statistics') — plain-language (~grade 8), fully provenanced, carrying no per-cancer medical claims, passing the content-safety lint and a plain-language review — the general health-literacy output of the project and the foundation for incidence-explorers-explainer-013.
